title: Release operations runbook (tag push, verify, publish)
audience: maintainer (Pedro), executed manually per release, not by impl agent

context.goal: Document the human steps to cut a versioned release once T11 workflow exists. Lives at docs/RELEASING.md.
context.trigger_model: Tag-driven. Push a tag vX.Y.Z, GH Actions builds and creates a draft Release, maintainer reviews and publishes.

prerequisites: T10 manual test matrix passed on the commit being tagged
release_steps: Run `cargo check` in src-tauri/ to refresh Cargo.lock with new version
Update CHANGELOG.md (create if absent) with notable changes since last tag
Commit: git add -A ; git commit -m 'release: vX.Y.Z'
Push commit to main: git push origin main
Tag: git tag -a vX.Y.Z -m 'Dictate vX.Y.Z' (annotated tag — required for GHA trigger and visibility)
Push tag: git push origin vX.Y.Z
Watch Actions tab: https://github.com/USERNAME/dictate/actions — wait ~15-25 min for both matrix legs
Once green, open https://github.com/USERNAME/dictate/releases — find draft 'Dictate vX.Y.Z'
Verify artifacts present (see verify_artifacts), edit release notes, untick 'Draft', click Publish
post_release_smoke: Download Windows .exe to a clean Win11 VM, install, dictate one sentence in Notepad — verify works
Download Linux .AppImage on a fresh Pop!_OS VM, chmod +x, run, dictate — verify works
Update README install one-liners if release URL pattern changed
Announce: post link to release page wherever relevant
rollback_procedure: If a critical bug is discovered post-publish: open the Release on GitHub, click 'Delete release' (this also removes the tag's association)
Optionally delete the git tag: git tag -d vX.Y.Z ; git push --delete origin vX.Y.Z
Fix bug, bump to vX.Y.Z+1 (do NOT reuse the broken version number — installers cache aggressively)
smartscreen_notes: Unsigned NSIS .exe will trigger 'Windows protected your PC' SmartScreen. Users must click 'More info' -> 'Run anyway'.
Reputation builds over downloads/installs over time, reducing the warning.
To eliminate: buy code signing cert (DigiCert/Sectigo, ~$200/yr), add WINDOWS_CERTIFICATE secret to repo, add signtool step to release.yml after Tauri build. Defer until release count justifies cost.
future_improvements: Auto-generate CHANGELOG from conventional commits (git-cliff or release-please)
Add tauri-plugin-updater + signing keys for in-app auto-updates
Add macOS to matrix (separate ticket — not in scope of Windows port)
steps: Create docs/RELEASING.md with content based on this ticket's structure
Link from README.md ## Roadmap or new ## Releasing section: 'Maintainer: see docs/RELEASING.md'
Run through the runbook end-to-end for v0.1.0 as the first real release
acceptance: docs/RELEASING.md exists and is followable by someone who has never released this project
v0.1.0 published on GitHub Releases with all 5 artifact types
Both install.ps1 and install.sh in README correctly point to USERNAME/dictate
